executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with over 100 Canadian organizations compromised in the past 48 hours across all critical sectors. The Qilin ransomware group leads the assault with 20+ victims including Canada Goose, an Eastern Townships school board, and the Workers Health & Safety Centre. Clop ransomware has hit 12 organizations, Play ransomware struck 10 victims, and 25+ additional ransomware families are actively exploiting Canadian targets. High-profile victims include National Money Mart (financial services, database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Municipality of North Perth, Kids & Company childcare, and Morison Insurance. This represents a coordinated, mass-exploitation event requiring immediate incident response across public and private sectors.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 20 Canadian organizations including Service Star Freightways, Canada Goose (via Coinbase Cartel association), Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra law firm, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG CPA, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a ransomware emergency with 100+ organizations compromised across all provinces and sectors in 48 hours. Critical infrastructure including public health (Peterborough Public Health), municipal government (Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart, Bais Yaakov), telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network), and public services (Workers Health & Safety Centre) have been breached. No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period despite the massive scale of the attacks.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (major retailer), Active Green + Ross, Canadian Tire/SportChek/other major retailers not identified in this batch
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (database leaked by Everest ransomware), Morison Insurance Brokers, Trucash, Axion50plus, Pewarchuk CPA, MG CPA, Reidhurst Nagy. Major banks (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) not identified as compromised

report_2w.executive_summary: Canada's cyber agency (CCCS) issued a critical alert on June 18 regarding FortiBleed—a leak of thousands of compromised Fortinet device credentials now being actively exploited. Separately, a massive data breach at Japanese telecom KDDI exposed 14.2 million email logins across six ISPs. Cisco Unified Communications Manager vulnerabilities are under active exploitation with a CISA-imposed Sunday deadline for patching. Multiple ransomware incidents hit U.S. healthcare and retail sectors, including JCPenney (368K records), Ralph Lauren, and medical device maker Hologic. Phishing campaigns targeting hotel front-desk systems in Europe/Asia and Signal backup credentials (FBI alert) are actively spreading.

items.description: PHPUnit vulnerability being actively scanned by Azure-based attackers
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.description: Use-after-free in getaddrinfo() causing memory corruption via spoofed DNS responses in Zephyr RTOS
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High
items.access_required: Network / Adjacent network position for DNS spoofing

items.name: FortiBleed Campaign Actors
items.targets: Organizations using Fortinet devices globally, including Canadian entities
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1133 (External Remote Services) - exploiting leaked credentials for initial access to Fortinet infrastructure

items.name: KARAWANG ERROR SYSTEM
items.targets: Karawang School System (Indonesia education institution)
items.ttps: T1589.002 (Gather Victim Identity Information: Email Addresses), T1567.002 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - data breach and dark web posting

items.name: Hotel Front-Desk Phishing Campaign
items.targets: Hotels in Europe and Asia
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), T1059.007 (Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript) - ZIP files with photos dropping Node.js implant since April 2026

items.name: Russian Messenger Service Espionage Campaign
items.targets: Messenger service users (SSU/FBI joint investigation)
items.ttps: T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), T1114 (Email Collection) - targeting messenger platforms for intelligence gathering

items.name: REDACT Ransomware Group
items.targets: Hologic (U.S. medical device manufacturer)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage) - ransomware with data exfiltration and dark web leak

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-014 on June 18, 2026, warning of the FortiBleed campaign—thousands of compromised Fortinet credentials actively being exploited to target organizations. This represents a critical threat to Canadian entities using Fortinet infrastructure for VPN and network security. No specific Canadian retail, financial, or critical infrastructure breaches were identified in this reporting period, though the FortiBleed advisory applies broadly to Canadian organizations.
